meta_title: For Founders: Git with AI to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Git with AI to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to use Git with AI to overcome a lack of portfolio. An intermediate case study using Antigravity and Claude Code to accelerate your SaaS launch.
content.answer_block: Building a portfolio from scratch requires demonstrating shipping velocity, not just static code. By integrating Git-based AI workflows with Antigravity and Claude Code, you can transform complex requirements into production-ready features in hours. This methodology proves your technical competency by delivering functional, revenue-ready SaaS components that serve as your ultimate portfolio.
content.intro:
The silent killer of every nascent SaaS founder is the 'portfolio trap.' You have the vision and the technical intuition, but the blank repository stares back, mocking your lack of a track record. Investors and early adopters don't care about your resume; they care about your ability to execute under pressure and deliver value.

Today, we pivot. We aren't just writing code; we are engineering a high-velocity production pipeline. By weaponizing Antigravity and Claude Code, you will bridge the gap between abstract ideas and a robust, version-controlled portfolio that screams 'Founder' rather than 'Student.' Let's turn your Git history into a masterclass of software delivery.

steps.title: Architecting the Core SaaS Foundation
steps.description: Define the MVP scope to ensure your Git history shows a logical, iterative progression rather than a messy initial commit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a CTO. Generate a project structure for a SaaS MVP focusing on modularity, clean architecture, and rapid feature iteration. Include a README that outlines the business value.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'initialize project scaffolding with modular architecture'

steps.title: Automating Feature Implementation
steps.description: Use AI to write the boilerplate and core logic, ensuring your code follows industry-standard patterns that look professional in a repo.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust authentication and user management module using secure practices. Ensure the code is production-ready and documented.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'implement user authentication service with JWT'

steps.title: Establishing High-Quality Git Hooks
steps.description: Configure automated testing and linting to ensure every commit in your portfolio represents high-quality, bug-free code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure Husky and lint-staged for this repository to enforce strict coding standards on every pre-commit.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'set up git hooks for automated linting and testing'

steps.title: Generating Documentation as a Product
steps.description: Documentation is the hallmark of a senior developer. Use AI to create technical documentation that explains the 'why' behind your design choices.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a technical deep-dive document for this codebase, explaining the architectural decisions and the scalability of the current stack.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'generate developer documentation and API specs'

steps.title: Deploying and Versioning for Impact
steps.description: Finalize your deployment pipeline to show you understand the full lifecycle of software, from local code to live production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that automates testing, building, and deployment to a staging environment on every push to main.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'configure CI/CD pipeline for automated deployment'

pro_tips: Commit with intent: Use conventional commits (feat:, fix:, chore:) to make your Git log readable for potential investors.
The 'Hidden' Readme: Your README.md is your landing page; treat it with the same design rigor as your actual UI.
Atomic Commits: Never push 'work in progress' blocks. Break features into small, logical, functional commits to show disciplined workflow.
Focus on Edge Cases: If you want to impress, use AI to write comprehensive error handling for every API endpoint you build.
Measure Velocity: Use your Git history to track how many features you ship per week, and highlight this metric in your founder bio.
content.next_steps: After completing this, publish your repo to GitHub, write a 'build log' blog post linking to the specific commits, and start networking by sharing your progress on Twitter/X to attract your first beta users.
content.conversion_block: Your portfolio is the byproduct of your output. Stop waiting for permission to be a founder and start building a history that speaks for itself. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
